email.subject: re: still waiting
email.body:
hi - circling back AGAIN on the thing from last month. nobody has gotten back to me.
this is the second time i'm asking. can someone please just tell me where my paperwork
is. getting frustrated tbh.
- Marcy

items.summary: Client is asking for an update on her paperwork and is frustrated.
items.draft:
Dear Marcy,

Thank you for reaching out. We apologize for the delay in responding and understand your frustration. We are currently reviewing your request and will get back to you as soon as possible with an update on your paperwork.

Best regards,
The Team
items.draft_rationale: The client's email indicates a high urgency and a document request. Since there are no specific figures to verify and the firm has no recorded amount, the draft acknowledges the request and promises a follow-up without committing to any specific details or timelines.

email.subject: Question about invoice #4471
email.body:
Hey team,
Got the invoice but the total looks off? I thought we agreed on the 2,400 number not
2,850. Can you double check. Also do you take ACH now or still just cards
thanks
Ray
Delgado Heating & Air

items.summary: Client has a question about invoice total and payment methods.
items.draft:
Dear Ray,

Thank you for reaching out regarding invoice #4471. We have received your email and are reviewing the total amount. Our records currently show a balance of $2400, and we will investigate the discrepancy you've noted. We will follow up with you shortly regarding this and your payment inquiry.

Best regards, The Team
items.draft_rationale: The flag contradicts_crm is true, so the draft acknowledges the client's message and notes a discrepancy with the firm's records rather than confirming the client's figure.

email.subject: URGENT need this today
email.body:
need the Q2 numbers pulled together for my 3pm call today. i know its short notice.
whatever you have is fine i just cant walk in empty handed. call my cell if easier
651-555-0148

signals: phone in body corroborates CRM phone

items.summary: Client needs Q2 numbers for a call today.
items.draft:
Dear Tina,

Thank you for reaching out. We understand you need Q2 financial information urgently for a call today.

While we are looking into this for you, we do not have a confirmed figure on file at this moment. We will review our records and aim to get this information to you as quickly as possible. Please note that any figures provided will be based on our verified records.

Best regards,
The Team
items.draft_rationale: The client's request for specific financial figures was not met because the firm has no recorded amount for this client, necessitating a conservative and non-committal response while emphasizing reliance on verified records.

email.body:
following up on the proposal. looks good overall. one thing - can we push the start to
july instead of june, kid's graduation stuff. lmk

items.summary: Client wants to push the start date to July.
items.draft:
Dear J. Park,

Thank you for your email and for reviewing the proposal. We understand you'd like to adjust the start date to July due to a personal commitment.

We have noted your request and will confirm the revised start date shortly. Our records currently show a service amount of $6,500, and we will reconcile this with your proposal details as we move forward.

Best regards,
The Team
items.draft_rationale: The draft acknowledges the client's request to change the start date and notes the firm's recorded amount, avoiding confirmation of any figure from the client's email. It prioritizes the client's request to adjust the start date while adhering to the rule of not confirming unverified financial figures.

email.subject: New client referral - pls add
email.body:
Hi, referring a new client over. Name is Tom Becker, runs a small roofing outfit out of
Eagan. tom.becker at beckerroofing dot com, phone is somewhere around 612 555 0193 i think.
He wants help with the bookkeeping cleanup we talked about. Can you reach out this week?
Thanks!!
Sandra

signals: sender is a referral, not the client
phone match (6125550193) on CRM row with no email
phone in body corroborates CRM phone

gemini adjudication: agree (high) — The email explicitly mentions referring a new client, Tom Becker, who runs Becker Roofing, and provides a phone number that matches the CRM entry, strongly indicating a correct match.

adjudication.reason: The email explicitly mentions referring a new client, Tom Becker, who runs Becker Roofing, and provides a phone number that matches the CRM entry, strongly indicating a correct match.

items.summary: Referral of new client Tom Becker for bookkeeping cleanup.
items.draft:
Dear Sandra,

Thank you for referring Tom Becker to us. We have received his contact information and will reach out to him this week to discuss his bookkeeping needs.

Best regards,
The Team

INTERNAL NOTE: We will reach out to Tom Becker to discuss bookkeeping cleanup.
items.draft_rationale: The sender is a referral, so the draft thanks the referrer and confirms outreach to the client. It avoids confirming any client-specific details as per the hard rules.

email.subject: complaint
email.body:
this is ridiculous. i was charged twice. TWICE. i want a refund and i want to know how
this happened. if i dont hear back by friday im disputing the charge with my bank.

items.summary: Client was charged twice and wants a refund.
items.draft:
Dear angryclient@protonmail.com,

Thank you for reaching out to us regarding a potential double charge. We understand your concern and are looking into this matter. As our records for your account are not immediately available for review, we will need a little time to investigate thoroughly. A member of our team will be in touch shortly to discuss this further.

Best regards,
The Team
items.draft_rationale: The client's status is 'churned or inactive', so the response adopts a re-engagement tone while acknowledging the billing issue and deferring specifics until a review is complete.

email.subject: checking in :)
email.body:
hi hope you're doing well! no rush at all but whenever you get a sec, did the new
policy documents ever come through? i looked in my email and dont see them. might be in
spam who knows lol. thanks so much!

items.summary: Client is checking if policy documents were sent.
items.draft:
Dear Bev,

Thank you for reaching out. We've checked our records and haven't yet received the policy documents. We'll look into this further and will reach out if we locate them or require anything further.

Best regards,
The Team
items.draft_rationale: The firm's records indicate that policy documents have not been received, so the draft politely acknowledges the client's query and states that the documents are still pending without confirming any figures or actions. The low urgency and lack of specific figures also support a conservative, non-committal response.

email.subject: Fwd: docs
email.body:
see below, this is everything the bank asked for. let me know if you need anything else.
attaching the 2023 and 2024 returns too once i find them.

---------- Forwarded message ----------
the loan officer needs P&L, balance sheet, and last two years returns by EOW

items.summary: Client is requesting documents needed for a loan.
items.draft:
Dear Carlos Morales,

Thank you for sending over the requested documents. We understand that the loan officer requires a P&L, balance sheet, and your last two years of returns by the end of the week. We are reviewing the information you've provided and will reach out if we have any questions or require further details. 

Best regards, The Team
items.draft_rationale: The 'contradicts_crm' and 'referenced_invoice_or_amount_not_in_crm' flags were false, but the client's email did not contain a specific figure that needed to be addressed. The draft focuses on acknowledging the document request and confirming the firm's understanding of the requirements without repeating any unverified client figures.

email.subject: cancel
email.body:
we need to cancel the meeting thursday something came up on a jobsite. can we do early
next week instead? mon or tues morning works. not wed.

items.summary: Client wants to reschedule a meeting.
items.draft:
Dear Dwight,

Thank you for letting us know about the need to reschedule your meeting. We understand that jobsite demands can arise unexpectedly.

We will reach out to coordinate a new time for early next week, likely Monday or Tuesday morning, and will avoid Wednesday.

Best regards,
The Team
items.draft_rationale: The draft is grounded in the matched client 'Dwight S / Stella Roofing' and the detected intent 'scheduling'. The firm's recorded amount is absent, so the draft avoids confirming any financial figure. The draft offers to reschedule for early next week as requested, and proposes Monday or Tuesday mornings while noting the unavailability of Wednesday.

email.subject: Re: Re: Re: onboarding
email.body:
ok i filled out the form but it wouldnt let me submit, kept saying the EIN field was
invalid? our EIN is 47-1839204. also you asked for prior bookkeeper contact - thats
just me, i did it myself before this. anything else you need from me?

items.summary: Client is having trouble submitting onboarding form due to invalid EIN.

Dear Priya,

Thank you for reaching out and for submitting the form. We apologize for the trouble with the EIN field submission; we've made a note of this error.

We have your information and will review it shortly. A member of our team will be in touch with next steps.

Best regards,
The Team
items.draft_rationale: The client reported a form submission error. The draft acknowledges the error and the client's message, states the firm is reviewing it, and promises a follow-up, aligning with the 'form submit error' firm note and the 'onboarding_lead' intent without confirming any unverified details.

email.subject: payment

sent the payment via zelle this morning for the march work. lmk when it lands. also when
do we get the next set of reports, end of month? want to make sure im budgeting right

items.summary: Client sent payment and is asking about next reports.
items.draft:
Hi Mike,

Thank you for your email. We've received your payment confirmation via Zelle for the March work and will process it accordingly. Our records currently show a balance of $1800 for this.

Regarding the reports, they are typically sent at the end of the month. We will ensure you receive them promptly.

Best regards,
The Team
items.draft_rationale: The sender is the client and there are no flags that contradict the firm's records or require a conservative approach, so the draft confirms receipt of payment and provides information about the reports while stating the firm's recorded amount.

email.subject: insurance question
email.body:
one of my carriers is asking for a loss run going back 5 years on the Hendricks account.
do you have that or do i need to pull it. honestly not even sure where it lives anymore.
this is the third carrier asking this month its exhausting

items.summary: Requesting 5 years of loss runs for Hendricks account.

Dear Ms. Whitaker,

Thank you for reaching out. We understand you are requesting a loss run for the Hendricks account going back five years.

Our team is looking into this and will follow up with you shortly regarding the availability of this document.

email.subject: hello
email.body:
saw your site. do you guys work with dental practices? we have 2 locations and our
books are a mess, like a year behind. what would something like that cost roughly

items.summary: Inquiry about bookkeeping services for a dental practice.
items.draft:
Dear newlead2024@yahoo.com,

Thank you for reaching out and for your interest in our services. We do have experience working with dental practices and understand that managing bookkeeping for multi-location businesses can be complex, especially when there's a backlog.

To provide you with an accurate estimate, we'll need a bit more information about your specific needs and the scope of work involved. A member of our team will reach out to you shortly to discuss this further and answer any questions you may have.

Best regards,
The Team
items.draft_rationale: The draft is a conservative response due to the lead's status and lack of firm records, acknowledging their situation and indicating a follow-up without committing to costs or specific services.

email.subject: RE: tax stuff
email.body:
quick one - my accountant retired and i need someone to take over before the extension
deadline in oct. i have everything in a shoebox basically (quickbooks but havent touched
it since feb). is that something you handle. dont judge me lol

signals: company "Greg's Auto Body" corroborates email domain

items.summary: Client needs new accountant to handle tax filing before extension deadline.
items.draft:
Dear Greg,

Thank you for reaching out. We understand you're looking for assistance with your bookkeeping and tax preparations before the October deadline, especially given your previous accountant's retirement. We do handle such transitions and can help organize your QuickBooks data.

To get started, we'd like to schedule a brief call to discuss your needs and how we can best support you. Please let us know what time works best for you.

Best regards,
The Team
items.draft_rationale: The draft addresses the client's stated need for onboarding and acknowledges the urgency due to the upcoming deadline and prior accountant's retirement. It aims for a warm, professional, and non-committal tone by focusing on scheduling a call rather than confirming figures or making specific commitments, given the client's informal data state.
